entries.check_message: Expected characters are: @ or %% or *.
entries.note: First substantive byte is an invalid top-level line start (`x`) before an otherwise ordinary CHAT file. CLAN emits CHECK 1; chatter rejects the bad line start with E316.

entries.check_message: Missing ':' character and argument.
entries.note: A complete CHAT preamble is followed by a main tier whose speaker code is not terminated by a colon. CLAN emits CHECK 2; chatter rejects the malformed line with E316.

entries.check_message: Missing either TAB or SPACE character.
entries.note: The character after the speaker-tier colon must be whitespace (check.cpp ~1756: !isSpace after check_FoundText); the fixture writes `*CHI:hello .` with no tab. CLAN emits CHECK 3; chatter rejects the line as unparsable content (E316).

entries.check_message: Found a space character instead of TAB character after Tier name
entries.note: A space instead of a TAB after the speaker code (*CHI: hi). CLAN 4; chatter requires the tab in-grammar and surfaces the recovery node as E316.

entries.check_message: Illegal colon on a header that takes no colon (@Begin).
entries.note: `@Begin:` (a stray colon on the no-colon @Begin header) parses as a valid @Begin followed by a stray `:` ERROR node. The streaming lowering silently dropped that nested ERROR node, so chatter accepted the file (Valid:1) while CLAN rejects it (codes 5 and 6). A whole-tree recovery-node backstop in parse_lines_with_old_tree now surfaces any surviving ERROR node as E316 UnparsableContent (recovery is not validity); the AST is still produced. CLAN CHECK 5 (co-emitted with 6).

entries.check_message: @Begin header missing or malformed.
entries.note: `@Begins` (a misspelled @Begin) parses as @Begin plus a trailing `s` ERROR node that the streaming lowering silently dropped. The whole-tree recovery-node backstop now surfaces it as E316. CLAN rejects it (codes 6 and 17). CLAN CHECK 6 (co-emitted with 17).

entries.check_message: "@End" is missing at the end of the file.
entries.note: File has no @End header at all. CLAN 7 (@End missing at end of file); chatter E502 MissingEndHeader.

entries.check_message: Expected characters are: @ %% * TAB.
entries.note: A body line that does not begin with @, %, * or TAB (free text). CLAN 8 (expected @ %% * TAB); chatter E326 surfaces the unparsable main-tier start.

entries.check_message: Tier name is longer than 1024.
entries.note: Speaker/tier name before the colon exceeds SPEAKERLEN=1024 characters (check.cpp:2395, first pass). chatter rejects via undeclared-speaker (E522/E308/W108) and speaker-ID-length E307 (max 7 chars), not via a dedicated raw length cap; CHECK aborts first pass (second pass not attempted).

entries.check_message: Symbol is not declared in the depfile.
entries.note: Documented divergence (Franklin, 2026-07-12): a depfile-membership check. A token undeclared in a corpus optional/global depfile is not universally invalid CHAT; chatter validates universal CHAT-format validity, never external-config membership (CHAT validity must never depend on any config file). chatter deliberately accepts.

entries.check_message: Missing speaker name and/or role.
entries.note: An @ID tier whose 3rd field (speaker code) or 8th field (role) is empty (check.cpp:5060/5084). E511 'ID header speaker field cannot be empty' is the direct equivalent. CLAN also emits 142 on the same fixture.

entries.check_message: Duplicate speaker declaration.
entries.note: Same speaker declared twice in @Participants. chatter E549 DuplicateSpeakerDeclaration.

entries.check_message: Duplicate speaker declaration.
entries.note: Two @ID lines naming the same speaker (CHI). Distinct from the @Participants-duplicate variant (CHECK_013_duplicate_speaker.cha): CLAN 13 fires for duplicate @ID lines too, which chatter previously missed. Now E549 via check_duplicate_id_headers. CLAN CHECK 13.

entries.check_message: Spaces before tier code.
entries.note: Leading spaces before the *tier code. CLAN 14 (also 4); chatter E326.

entries.check_message: Illegal role. Please see "depfile.cut" for list of roles.
entries.note: An @ID tier whose role field does not match any role listed in the depfile @Participants template (check.cpp:5087). E532 'Invalid participant role' is the direct equivalent. CLAN also emits 142 on the same fixture.

entries.check_message: Tier is not declared in depfile file.
entries.note: Undeclared dependent tier %zzz: (neither %x* nor a chatter-supported standard tier). chatter rejects it via E605 UnsupportedDependentTier (Error severity). The supported set deliberately excludes the legacy %grt/%tra/%trn/%umor tiers retired with the UD-%mor consolidation, so chatter is intentionally stricter than CLAN there (documented divergence). %x* is always valid. CLAN CHECK 17.

entries.check_message: Speaker @ID is not specified in a participants list.
entries.note: An @ID tier whose speaker code is not declared on the @Participants tier (check.cpp:5062). E523 '@ID header for X but speaker not in @Participants' is the direct equivalent. Note CLAN prints the literal tier name '@ID' in the message (quirk of check_mess case 18 printing utterance->speaker).

entries.check_message: Or a SPACE should be added after it.
entries.note: A token (here the retrace code '[/]') is immediately followed by a non-space, non-delimiter character with no space between (check.cpp:4590/4592); also fires for a time bullet glued to a word. Full CLAN message is two lines: 'Illegal use of delimiter in a word.' + 'Or a SPACE should be added after it.(19)'. chatter accepts '[/]x' glued (exit 0). Grounded also with a bullet glued to a word (u019d.cha).

entries.check_message: Undeclared suffix in depfile.
entries.note: Documented divergence (Franklin, 2026-07-12): a depfile-membership check. A token undeclared in a corpus optional/global depfile is not universally invalid CHAT; chatter validates universal CHAT-format validity, never external-config membership (CHAT validity must never depend on any config file). chatter deliberately accepts.

entries.check_message: Utterance delimiter expected.
entries.note: A main tier with text but no utterance terminator (check.cpp:4921; tier UTD==1, no delimiter found). E305 is chatter's missing-terminator error.

entries.check_message: Unmatched [ found on the tier.
entries.note: An unclosed replacement scope: `dog [: cat` opens `[:` with no closing `]`. CLAN 22 (unmatched [) (also 11/48/106); chatter rejects with E311. A stray `[`+space alone is CLAN 48, not 22; the bracket must open a real scope to be 'unmatched'.

entries.check_message: Unmatched ] found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched ] on a main tier. CLAN 23; chatter rejects via E375 (content-annotation parse error).

entries.check_message: Unmatched < found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched < on a main tier (open angle, no close). CLAN 24 (also 160); chatter surfaces the recovery node as E316. NOTE: earlier wrongly recorded as not-firing; that was a one-off CLAN depfile-load miss, the helper now guards against it.

entries.check_message: Unmatched > found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched > on a main tier. CLAN 25; chatter surfaces the tree-sitter recovery node as E316 UnparsableContent.

entries.check_message: Unmatched { found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched { on a main tier. CLAN 26 (also 11, 48); chatter E316.

entries.check_message: Unmatched } found on the tier.
entries.note: Unmatched } on a main tier. CLAN 27 (also 48); chatter surfaces the recovery node as E316 UnparsableContent.

entries.check_message: Missing text after the colon.
entries.note: A declared tier with a colon but no text after it (check.cpp:1748); '@' headers are exempt ('to always allow empty header tiers'), so the fixture uses an empty dependent tier '%fac:'. E330 'Tier has empty content' is the direct equivalent. An empty '*CHI:' main tier instead yields CLAN (21)+(70), not (31).

entries.check_message: Code is not declared in depfile.
entries.note: Documented divergence (Franklin, 2026-07-12): a depfile-membership check. A token undeclared in a corpus optional/global depfile is not universally invalid CHAT; chatter validates universal CHAT-format validity, never external-config membership (CHAT validity must never depend on any config file). chatter deliberately accepts.

entries.check_message: Illegal date representation.
entries.note: @Date is not a valid date ('notadate'). CLAN 34 (illegal date representation); chatter E518 InvalidDateFormat.

entries.check_message: Illegal time representation.
entries.note: @Time Duration 99:99:99 is shape-valid (HH:MM:SS) but out of clock range. chatter previously validated the shape only; now TimeDurationValue::has_out_of_range_component flags components outside hours 0-23 / minutes,seconds 0-59 and emits E540. CLAN range rule grounded empirically: 25:00:00, 99:30:00, 12:60:00, 12:00:60 all CLAN 35; 23:59:59 OK. CLAN CHECK 35.

entries.check_message: Utterance delimiter must be at the end of the utterance.
entries.note: Utterance-delimiter followed by more material on the main tier (check.cpp:4394/4396; DelFound set, then a non-bullet, non-[ token appears). E316 (unparsable/misplaced content) rejects the same shape.

entries.check_message: Undeclared prefix.
entries.note: Documented divergence (Franklin, 2026-07-12): a depfile-membership check. A token undeclared in a corpus optional/global depfile is not universally invalid CHAT; chatter validates universal CHAT-format validity, never external-config membership (CHAT validity must never depend on any config file). chatter deliberately accepts.

entries.check_message: Numbers should be written out in words.
entries.note: A word starting with a digit on a main tier (excluding '0'-forms) that matches no depfile number pattern (check.cpp:4811 via check_matchnumber). E220 is chatter's digits-in-word error. CLAN also emits (47) on the same fixture.

entries.check_message: Use either "&" or "()", but not both.
entries.note: An '&'-initial filler word containing '(' (check.cpp:4824). CLAN also emits (65) and (11) on the fixture; chatter E207 rejects the '&ab(c)' form.

entries.check_message: The file must start with "@Begin" tier.
entries.note: No @Begin header. chatter rejects with E504 (Missing required @Begin header in file preamble). Grounded vs real CLAN CHECK 43; audit name-matcher had marked it a gap.

entries.check_message: The file must end with "@End" tier.
entries.note: Speaker content after the @End tier. CLAN 44 (file must end with @End); chatter flags E502 MissingEndHeader (and E501). Both reject the file.

entries.check_message: There were more @Bg than @Eg tiers found.
entries.note: At end of file, one or more @Bg tiers were never closed by a matching @Eg (check.cpp:5758); CHECK lists the unmatched @Bg contents after the message. E526 is chatter's unmatched-@Bg error.

entries.check_message: This @Eg does not have matching @Bg.
entries.note: An @Eg tier with no open @Bg carrying the same text (check.cpp:5391/5400). E527 is chatter's unmatched-@Eg error.

entries.check_message: Numbers are not allowed inside words.
entries.note: A digit inside a word (he11o). CLAN 47 (numbers not allowed inside words); chatter E220.

entries.check_message: Illegal character(s) '|' found.
entries.note: A standalone illegal character on a main tier; '|' between words hits one of the 22 check_err(48,...) sites in the word scanner. chatter accepts '*CHI:\thello | there .' (exit 0). CLAN also emits (11) on the fixture. 48 has 22 call sites; this grounds only the bare-pipe shape; other illegal chars ('#' inside a word, '}' etc.) also produce (48).

entries.check_message: Redundant utterance delimiter.
entries.note: A second utterance delimiter after one was already found on a main tier (check.cpp:4351/4354/4554). E316 rejects 'hello . .'.

entries.check_message: expected [ ]; < > should be followed by [ ]
entries.note: CLAN prints this message WITHOUT a (51) suffix (format string lacks (%d)); grounding matches by message text. A '<...>' angle group not followed by a '[...]' code, detected at end of tier or at the next word (check.cpp:4376/4568, anb flag from check_CheckBrakets). QUIRK: CLAN prints this message WITHOUT the numeric '(51)' suffix; check_mess case 51 (check.cpp) has no (%d) in its format string. Grounding is the verbatim unique message text. chatter frames the same construct as an incomplete retra

entries.check_message: Item '[//]' must be preceded by text.
entries.note: A '[' code that requires preceding text ([//], [: ...], [/], ["], [<]) appearing as the first item of an utterance (check.cpp:4859; word[1] in <,>,:,/,"). E747 (retrace marker without preceding material) is the direct equivalent.

entries.check_message: Only one "@Begin" can be in a file.
entries.note: Two @Begin headers. chatter rejects with E501 (Duplicate @Begin header). Behavioral grounding overturns the name-matcher audit, which marked this a gap.

entries.check_message: Unmatched ( found in the word.
entries.note: More '(' than ')' inside a single word (check.cpp:4571, pb>lpb after word scan). chatter rejects 'ab(c d .' via unparsable-content/terminator errors.

entries.check_message: Unmatched ) found in the word.
entries.note: More ')' than '(' inside a single word (check.cpp:4572, pb<lpb). chatter rejects 'ab)c d .' via unparsable-content/terminator errors.

entries.check_message: Please add space between word and pause symbol: '('.
entries.note: Pause '(' opening directly at the end of a word (check.cpp 4437). GAP CLOSED 2026-07-09: model validation check_pause_glued_to_word (span adjacency, utterance/spacing.rs) + re2c word-then-pause token-window mirror. Spec: E751_pause_glued_to_word.md. CLAN also emits (48) on the same fixture; expected_chatter_codes are subset-asserted so E751 alone is the pinned signal. ADJUDICATED (maintainer decision, 2026-07-10): NOT a pause-spacing rule; in our AST '(.)' is not a legal shortening ('.' is not a word character), so word-glued '(.)' is a PARSE error in nature. Rejection kept; refactor queued to move the diagnostic from the validation layer to the parse layer.

A time bullet (\x15) opened but never closed before end of scan; hidenc stays TRUE at word end (check.cpp:4049/4797). Fixture: '%pho:\thello \x15123' with no closing \x15. QUIRK: on the unix build CLAN prints the error banner with an EMPTY message line: check_mess case 59's printf is inside '#if _MAC_CODE/#elif _WIN32', so no (59) trailer and no message text ever appear. Grounded banner_only: run restricted to '+e59' (block prints) and cross-checked '+e1' (clean), re-confirmed 2026-07-09.

entries.check_message: "@ID:" tier is missing in the file. Please run "insert" in Commands window on this data file.
entries.note: File has no @ID header. CLAN 60; chatter E522 (missing @ID header).

entries.check_message: "@Participants:" tier is expected here.
entries.note: No @Participants header. chatter rejects with E504 (Missing required @Participants header), plus E543/E522. Grounded vs real CLAN CHECK 61.

entries.check_message: Missing language information.
entries.note: An @ID tier with an empty language (first) field (check.cpp:5054; also 2536 for an empty 'word@s:' language marker and 5023). E510 is chatter's empty-@ID-language error. CLAN prints (62) twice on the fixture.

entries.check_message: Missing Corpus name.
entries.note: Empty corpus field (2nd) in @ID. chatter E514 EmptyIDCorpus. Root-caused: corpus was wrongly Option<CorpusName>; made it a required CorpusName like its siblings, the Validate trait flags empty. CLAN CHECK 63.

entries.check_message: Wrong gender information (Choose: female or male).
entries.note: @ID sex field is neither 'male' nor 'female' ('x'). CLAN 64; chatter E542 UnsupportedSex. Requires CLAN's depfile loaded (auto-resolved from the lib dir).

entries.check_message: Item '@' can not be followed by the next symbol.
entries.note: A special-form or morph marker followed by an illegal next symbol; 'hello@' with '@' at end of word (w[s+1]==EOS) hits check.cpp:3499-region (one of 31 sites). CLAN also emits (147) undeclared special form. This grounds only the trailing-@ shape of 65's 31 sites. chatter rejects via E202/E305.

entries.check_message: Or a SPACE should be added before it.
entries.note: An '&' in the middle of a word followed by a letter (not in the allowed set -,',&,+,^,~,#,@,),",_) (check.cpp:3232). Full CLAN message: 'Illegal character '&' in a word.' + 'Or a SPACE should be added before it.(66)'. E207 rejects 'ab&cd'. Sites 3311/3329 ()- and --shapes not separately grounded.

entries.check_message:
Item '+' must be followed by text, 
 preceded by SPACE or be removed.
entries.note: A word-final '+' (or '-') followed by end-of-word / certain punctuation must be followed by text (check.cpp 3319/3338; the CA-char site 3025 is inside #ifndef UNX). Grounded via 'hey+ .'; CLAN message spans two output lines, recorded with embedded newline.

entries.check_message: PARTICIPANTS TIER IS MISSING "CHI Target_Child".
entries.note: DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE (maintainer decision, 2026-07-08): this CHECK rule is option-gated (fires only under the non-default CLAN flags recorded in clan_flags), and default-mode CHECK accepts the fixture. Policy: option-gated CHECK rules do not bind parity; chatter intentionally accepts. Original grounding: OPTION-GATED (fires only under these CLAN flags; whether off-by-default CHECK rules bind parity is an open maintainer policy question). With the +g2 option (NAME_ROLE genopt, OFF by default), @Participants lacking a 'CHI Target_Child' entry triggers 68 unless '@Options: notarget' is present (check.cpp

entries.check_message: The UTF8 header is missing. If you edit and save the file, it will be inserted.
entries.note: File has no @UTF8 header. CLAN 69; chatter E503 MissingUTF8Header.

entries.check_message: Expected either text or "0" on this tier.
entries.note: A speaker tier containing no text word (only delimiters/pauses/brackets and not the '0' placeholder) at tier end (check.cpp 4984, isTextFound never set). Fixture '*CHI:\t.'; chatter rejects with three codes including E306 (no meaningful content).

entries.check_message: Item '[/]' must be before pause (#).
entries.note: Documented divergence (Franklin, 2026-07-12): the ordering rule keys on the # pause marker, which is legacy CLAN and not a live construct in chatter (chatter uses (.)/(..)/(...)); the rule references a construct chatter does not have. chatter deliberately accepts.

entries.check_message: Item '(.)' must precede the utterance delimiter or CA delimiter.
entries.note: A pause '(...)' or non-postcode '[...]' item appearing after the utterance delimiter (check.cpp 4740/4744). Fixture 'hey . (.)'; chatter rejects (unparsable content after terminator).

entries.check_message: Item '[x 2]' must be preceded by text or '0'.
entries.note: A bracketed code as the first item of a tier with nothing before it (also bullets not preceded by text; multiple sites: check.cpp 4045, 4781, 4861; CA sites 3030/3033 are #ifndef UNX). Fixture '[x 2] hey .'; chatter rejects (E375 repetition-count parse). CLAN additionally emits (11).

entries.check_message: Item '[+ exc]' must follow after utterance delimiter.
entries.note: A postcode '[+ ...]' occurring before the utterance delimiter (check.cpp 4747). Fixture 'hey [+ exc] .'; chatter rejects (postcode only legal after terminator).

entries.check_message: Only one letter is allowed with '@l'.
entries.note: An '@l' letter-marker word whose stem is more than one letter (check.cpp 2532, check_isOneLetter). Fixture 'ab@l .'; chatter accepts (exit 0). The one-letter constraint on @l is a gap.

entries.check_message: "@Languages:" tier is expected here.
entries.note: File has no @Languages header. CLAN 77 (also 61); chatter E504 MissingRequiredHeader. chatter uses one generic missing-required-header code where CLAN has per-header codes; parity is on validity.

entries.check_message: Item '+' must be used at the beginning of tier.
entries.note: A special delimiter word '+,' '+"' '+^' '+<' '++' (exactly two chars) appearing after the first word of the tier (check.cpp 4769). Fixture 'hey +, you .'; chatter rejects (unparsable ' +').

entries.check_message: Only one occurrence of | symbol per word is allowed.
entries.note: A %mor word with two | separators (n|dog|cat). CLAN 79 (only one | per word); chatter surfaces the malformed %mor structure as E316.

entries.check_message: There must be at least one occurrence of '|'.
entries.note: A %mor word with no | separator (bare 'dog'). CLAN 80 (must be at least one |); chatter E316 (also E702).

entries.check_message: BEG mark of bullet must be smaller than END mark.
entries.note: A media bullet whose END time is <= its BEG time (check.cpp 3879, new-format path with @Media header). Bullet \x15500_100\x15. Note @Media file name must match the .cha basename or CLAN adds (157) noise.

entries.check_message: Current BEG time is smaller than previous' tier BEG time
entries.note: A tier's first bullet BEG earlier than the previous tier's first-bullet BEG (check_tierBegTime), reachable only from the third bulleted tier on because tEndTime==0 skips the block on the first (check.cpp 3885/3890). Needs three tiers (1000_2000, 3000_4000, then 2500_2600): with only two, the tracking variable is still 0 and no error fires; a two-tier same-speaker attempt yields (133) instead. CLAN message has no trailing period.

entries.check_message: Current BEG time is smaller than previous' tier END time by 500 msec.
entries.note: DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE (maintainer decision, 2026-07-08): this CHECK rule is option-gated (fires only under the non-default CLAN flags recorded in clan_flags), and default-mode CHECK accepts the fixture. Policy: option-gated CHECK rules do not bind parity; chatter intentionally accepts. Original grounding: OPTION-GATED (fires only under these CLAN flags; whether off-by-default CHECK rules bind parity is an open maintainer policy question). With +c0 (checkBullets==1, OFF by default): current tier's bullet BEG earlier than previous tier's END (overlap) (check.cpp 3901). OPTION-GATED: emitted only under 'c

entries.check_message: Gap found between current BEG time and previous' tier END time.
entries.note: DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE (maintainer decision, 2026-07-08): this CHECK rule is option-gated (fires only under the non-default CLAN flags recorded in clan_flags), and default-mode CHECK accepts the fixture. Policy: option-gated CHECK rules do not bind parity; chatter intentionally accepts. Original grounding: OPTION-GATED (fires only under these CLAN flags; whether off-by-default CHECK rules bind parity is an open maintainer policy question). With +c0 (checkBullets==1, OFF by default): gap between current tier's bullet BEG and previous tier's END (check.cpp 3905). OPTION-GATED: emitted only under 'check +c

entries.check_message: Illegal character. Please re-enter it using Unicode standard.
entries.note: A Unicode Private Use Area character (U+E000) inside a word. CLAN 86 via isIllegalASCII (check.cpp:2880): 3-byte UTF-8 codepoints U+E000..U+FFFF except U+F170..U+F264 (CLAN-internal) and U+FF01..U+FF5E (fullwidth ASCII); U+00B7 allowed. chatter rejects it via E243 IllegalCharactersInWord (check_word_characters extends its illegal-char scan to the private-use ranges).

entries.check_message: Malformed structure.
entries.note: A %mor/%xmor/%trn (or %cnl) word whose compound structure is malformed: a '+' not in canonical 'pfx|+a|b' shape drives numOfCompounds to -2 or 0 (check.cpp 3592/3646). Fixture '%mor: n|cat+n|fish .' (compound missing the '|+' marker after the head POS). chatter rejects (E600 %mor item structure).

entries.check_message: Missing or extra or wrong characters found in bullet.
entries.note: A new-format bullet whose content is not digits_digits (e.g. wrong separator) (check.cpp 3863, res==2 from check_getMediaTagInfo). Bullet \x15100x200\x15 with @Media header. chatter rejects.

entries.check_message: Illegal time representation inside a bullet.
entries.note: A bullet time component written with a leading zero before another digit, e.g. 012 (check.cpp check_getMediaTagInfo res==3, call site 3865). Fixture: \x15012_200\x15 with @Media header. GAP CLOSED 2026-07-09: chatter now emits E748 at parse time from the raw component text (both bullet paths in talkbank-parser media_bullet.rs; token scan in talkbank-parser-re2c file.rs). Spec: E748_leading_zero_bullet_time.md. A bare 0 stays legal. ADJUDICATED (maintainer decision, 2026-07-10): STYLE error. Leading-zero bullet times are unambiguous; kept as an error by project policy: style violations are errors.

entries.check_message: Blank lines are not allowed.
Item ',' must be followed by space or end-of-line.
entries.note: Comma not followed by space or end-of-line on a speaker tier (check.cpp 4309-4320, with CA-mark exemptions). GAP CLOSED 2026-07-09: model validation check_comma_glued_to_next (span adjacency over walk_content; dummy spans skipped) plus a re2c token-stream mirror. Spec: E749_comma_glued_to_next_word.md. Narrowed to word-next: constructs placing their own character after the comma are exempt, matching CLAN. ADJUDICATED (maintainer decision, 2026-07-10): STYLE error. Comma-glue is unambiguous under our grammar; kept as an error by project policy: style violations are errors.

entries.check_message: Item '[' must be preceded by SPACE.
entries.note: '[' immediately preceded by '>' or ']' with no space (check.cpp 4469/4472; CA start-marks site 3179 is #ifndef UNX). Fixture '<hey>[/] hey .'; CLAN also emits (161). chatter rejects.

entries.check_message: Mismatch of speaker and %mor: utterance delimiters.
entries.note: %mor tier whose utterance delimiter differs from the speaker tier's recorded delimiter (check.cpp 4338 simple delimiters; 4522 '+...'-style). '*CHI: hey .' with '%mor: co|hey ?'. chatter rejects with the dedicated E716.

entries.check_message: Illegal character inside parentheses.
entries.note: '@' encountered inside parentheses within a word on a speaker tier (check.cpp 3360, paransFound). Fixture 'c(a@b)t .'; CLAN also emits (147). chatter rejects.

entries.check_message: Space is not allow in media file name inside bullets.
entries.note: An old-format bullet (no @Media header) '\x15%snd:"NAME"_B_E\x15' whose media file name contains a space (check.cpp 3923 via res==4; also reachable in the new-format path 3867). chatter rejects the old-style %snd bullet outright (E360), so anything CHECK flags inside it is also rejected; parity by strictness.

entries.check_message: Extension is not allow at the end of media file name.
entries.note: Media file name ending with an extension: in old-format bullets (check.cpp 3925 via res==5, used here), in new-format path (3869), or on the @Media header (5589). Grounded via old-format bullet '\x15%snd:"test.wav"_100_200\x15'; chatter rejects the %snd bullet (E360). The @Media-header variant of (99) was not separately grounded.

entries.check_message: Commas at the end of PARTICIPANTS tier are not allowed.
entries.note: Trailing comma at end of @Participants. The grammar already surfaces the dangling comma as a tree-sitter ERROR node inside the participants header; the parser reports it as E550 TrailingCommaInParticipants (parse-stage diagnostic). CLAN CHECK 100.

entries.check_message: Italic markers are no longer legal in CHAT.
entries.note: Any character carrying the italic text attribute, encoded in the file as ATTMARKER 0x02 + 0x03 (italic start) / 0x02 + 0x04 (italic end) (check.cpp: multiple is_italic() sites, e.g. 4289/4433). Fixture embeds raw bytes \x02\x03hey\x02\x04; CLAN emits (102) twice (once per attributed char run). chatter rejects the control bytes as unparsable, so legacy attribute encoding cannot pass.

entries.check_message: Illegal use of both CA and IPA on "@Options:" tier.
entries.note: @Options header (which must immediately follow @Participants) listing both 'CA' and 'IPA' (check.cpp 5478/5537). First attempt placed @Options after @ID and got first-pass (125) instead; @Options must sit right after @Participants. CLAN also emits (11) because IPA is not in the depfile @Options template. chatter rejects with E534.

entries.check_message: A code must be entirely on one line.
entries.note: A scoped code split across a newline (`[% comment` then a continuation line `continued]`) leaves an ERROR node in the main-tier content that the streaming lowering did not scan. The whole-tree recovery-node backstop now surfaces it as E316. CLAN CHECK 106.

entries.check_message: Only single commas are allowed in tier.
entries.note: Three or more consecutive commas on a speaker tier (check.cpp 4322; two commas give (156) instead). Fixture 'hey ,,, you .'; CLAN also emits (156). chatter rejects (E258 twice).

entries.check_message: Postcodes must precede the final bullet.
entries.note: A postcode `[+ trn]` placed after the final time bullet leaves an ERROR node in the utterance that the streaming lowering did not scan. The whole-tree recovery-node backstop now surfaces it as E316. CLAN rejects it (codes 108 and 112). CLAN CHECK 108 (co-emitted with 112).

entries.check_message: Postcodes are not allowed on dependent tiers.
entries.note: A postcode-shaped token `[+ trn]` on a dependent tier (%com). CLAN CHECK 109 ("Postcodes are not allowed on dependent tiers", check_CheckWords, check.cpp:3471-3690) flags it on any non-%x dependent tier. chatter does not model postcodes on dependent tiers: ordinary dependent tiers like %com parse as TextTier with no Postcode AST node, so there is nothing typed to detect, and flagging it would require a banned raw-text scan. Empirically (2026-06-25) CLAN's own analysis tools do not choke on dependent-tier postcodes: FREQ and MLU exclude the `[+ ...]` token from counts exactly as on the main tier, KWAL displays the line; only CHECK flags it. CHECK 109 is a CLAN-internal pedantic check, not a CHAT-validity rule, so chatter deliberately diverges (accepts) per the parity mandate's 'unless CHECK is buggy / not a real validity rule' clause. Divergence (chatter intentionally looser), not a gap.

entries.check_message: No bullet found on this tier.
entries.note: DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE (maintainer decision, 2026-07-08): this CHECK rule is option-gated (fires only under the non-default CLAN flags recorded in clan_flags), and default-mode CHECK accepts the fixture. Policy: option-gated CHECK rules do not bind parity; chatter intentionally accepts. Original grounding: OPTION-GATED (fires only under these CLAN flags; whether off-by-default CHECK rules bind parity is an open maintainer policy question). With +cN (checkBullets nonzero, OFF by default): a speaker tier containing no bullet at all (check.cpp 4915). OPTION-GATED: emitted only under 'check +c1' (or +c0); d

entries.check_message: Illegal pause format. Pause has to have '.'
entries.note: An illegal pause format: `(5)` (digits with no dot) instead of (.)/(..)/(...)/(n.). CLAN 111 (pause must have '.'); chatter rejects with E209 (also E220).

entries.check_message: Missing @Media: tier with media file name in headers section at the top of the file.
entries.note: A new-format digits_digits bullet in a file with NO @Media header (check.cpp 3927 via check_getOLDMediaTagInfo res==6). chatter accepts time bullets without an @Media header (exit 0). GAP: bullets should require an @Media header. Note the reference-table message has '%s' filled as '@Media:' in real output. CLOSED 2026-07-15 per the 2026-07-14 MEANINGFUL ruling: chatter rejects timing-without-@Media as E752 (inverse of E544); spec spec/errors/E752_timing_without_media.md.

entries.check_message: Illegal keyword, use "audio", "video" or look in depfile.cut.
entries.note: A comma-separated field after the file name on @Media that is not a depfile-listed keyword (audio/video/unlinked/...). E536 is the same rule (Unsupported @Media status: 'junkword'). E531 is extra chatter strictness: media filename 'test' vs fixture file name.

entries.check_message: Add "audio", "video" or look in depfile.cut for more keywords after the media file name on @Media: tier.
entries.note: @Media names a media file but lists neither 'audio' nor 'video'; CLAN emits (114) on the @Media line. FIXTURE REPAIRED 2026-07-09: the survey-batch copy was truncated mid-@Media-line (no utterance, no @End), so CLAN aborted first-pass with (7) and could never reach 114; the drift guard caught it. chatter rejects the keywordless @Media at parse time (E316 unparsable header line; an incidental E747 blank-line cascade fires on the newline the recovery leaves behind, deliberately not asserted).

entries.check_message: Old bullets format found. Please run "fixbullets" program to fix this data.
entries.note: With an @Media header present, a time bullet whose content starts with %s/%m, i.e. the old '%snd:"file"_beg_end' bullet format. Old-format bullet is unparsable to chatter (E316) and the file then has no timing info (E544). E531 is fixture-name noise.

entries.check_message: Specifying Font for individual lines is illegal. Please open this file and save it again.
entries.note: A bracketed code matching pattern [%fnt: *:*] (per-line font specification) on a tier. chatter rejects via E375 (failed to parse percent_annotation token '[%fnt: ...'), a parse-level rejection of the same construct.

entries.check_message: This character must be used in pairs. See if any are unmatched.
entries.note: A pairwise CA marker (faster U+2206, slower, creaky, whisper, left-arrow-circle U+21AB, etc.) occurring an odd number of times on a main tier. Dedicated chatter code: E230 'Unbalanced CA delimiter (Faster): missing closing delimiter'. CLAN tracks 15 pair-wise marker types; fixture uses faster U+2206.

entries.check_message: Missing word after code.
entries.note: Retrace marker [/] with nothing after it. chatter E370 StructuralOrderError.

entries.check_message: Please use three letter language code.
entries.note: A 2-letter (or 2-letter plus dash subtag) language code where a 3-letter ISO-639 code is expected, on @Languages, the @ID language field, [- lng] precodes, or @s codes. Dedicated chatter code: E519 "Language code 'en' should be 3 characters (got 2)". Fixture uses en consistently on @Languages and @ID.

entries.check_message: Language code "qqq" not found in "CLAN/lib/fixes/ISO-639.cut" file.
entries.note: A language code (right length) that is not present in CLAN/lib/fixes/ISO-639.cut, on @Languages or the @ID language field. GAP: chatter accepts 'qqq' when @Languages and @ID agree. chatter validates code shape (3 chars, E519) and @ID/@Languages consistency, but not membership in the ISO-639 inventory. An earlier fixture with @ID eng vs @Languages qqq was rejected only for the mismatch (incidental E519), so the sharp consistent fixture is recorded.

entries.check_message: Language on @ID tier is not defined on "@Languages:" header tier.
entries.note: @ID declares language 'eng' that is not present in the @Languages header (which declares 'fra'). CLAN 122; chatter E519. NOTE: code 122 has n_call_sites=0 in the generated catalogue (emitted via an indirect call the literal check_err grep misses) but IS emittable: verified vs real file-mode CLAN. The 8-code 'dead' list therefore needs empirical re-verification.

entries.check_message: Illegal character '<space>' found in tier text. If it CA, then add "@Options: CA"
entries.note: In a non-CA file (no @Options: CA), the tab after the speaker colon is followed by a space (CA-style alignment). GAP: chatter accepts '*CHI:<tab><space>dog .'. Second call site (check.cpp:1673, space at i==1 after leading tab) is logically unreachable (outer guard requires uttline[0] != tab); the live site is 1669.

entries.check_message: Please remove "unlinked" from @Media header.
entries.note: @Media declares `unlinked` but the main tier has a time bullet, so the media is in fact linked. chatter now emits E552 MediaUnlinkedWithTiming via check_media_unlinked_has_no_timing, the inverse of the existing E544 (linkage without timing). Grounded empirically: unlinked+bullet -> CLAN 124; unlinked+no-bullet and linked+bullet -> no 124. CLAN CHECK 124.

entries.check_message: "@Options" header must immediately follow "@Participants:" header.
entries.note: @Options placed after @ID instead of immediately after @Participants. chatter E551 OptionsHeaderOutOfOrder, mirroring the @ID rule (E548/CHECK 126). CLAN CHECK 125.

entries.check_message: "@ID" header must immediately follow "@Participants:" or "@Options" header.
entries.note: A changeable header (@Comment) sits between @Participants and the @ID block.

entries.check_message: Header must follow "@ID:" or "@Birth of" or "@Birthplace of" or "@L1 of" header.
entries.note: A changeable header sits between the @ID block and @Birth of.

entries.check_message: Unmatched ‹ found on the tier.
entries.note: Group-start marker U+2039 opened on a main tier and never closed (gm > 0 at end of tier). No dedicated code; unmatched opener makes the tier unparsable (E316).

entries.check_message: Unmatched › found on the tier.
entries.note: Group-end marker U+203A encountered with no open U+2039 (gm goes negative). Rejected at parse level: E316 unparsable content on the stray closer plus E305 missing terminator.

entries.check_message: Unmatched 〔 found on the tier.
entries.note: Sign-group-start marker U+3014 opened and never closed (sgm > 0 at end of tier). No dedicated code; rejected as unparsable (E316).

entries.check_message: Unmatched 〕 found on the tier.
entries.note: Sign-group-end marker U+3015 with no opener (sgm goes negative). Rejected at parse level (E316 + E305).

entries.check_message: Tabs should only be used to mark the beginning of lines.
entries.note: A tab in the middle of a main tier. chatter rejects it (E370, unexpected tab). Grounded vs real CLAN CHECK 132.

entries.check_message: BEG time is smaller than same speaker's previous END time by 4000 msec.
entries.note: With linked media, a bullet whose BEG time is more than 500 ms earlier than the same speaker's previous bullet END time. Dedicated chatter code: E704 'Speaker CHI overlaps with self: utterance 1 ends at 5000ms...'. E531 is fixture-name noise. CLAN threshold is >500ms (check.cpp:3892-3896); chatter's exact threshold not probed by this survey.

entries.check_message: Item 'unk|xxx' is illegal. Please run "mor" command on this data.
entries.note: A %mor/%trn (or %cnl) item of the form unk|xxx, unk|yyy, or *|www (mor output for untranscribed material). chatter rejects via alignment (E706: main tier has 0 alignable items, %mor has 1) because xxx is not %mor-alignable; there is no dedicated unk|xxx rule. A hypothetical fixture where counts still align was not probed.

entries.check_message: Item 'xx' is illegal.
entries.note: A main-tier word that is exactly 'xx' or 'yy' (case-insensitive); %cnl tiers also flag bare 'gen' and '^'. Dedicated chatter code: E241 '"xx" is not legal; did you mean to use "xxx"?'. Only the main-tier xx call site (check.cpp:3138) was grounded; the %cnl sites (3613, 3632) were not separately probed.

entries.check_message: Unmatched “ found on the tier.
entries.note: Open curly quote U+201C on a tier with no matching close quote (qt > 0 at end of tier). Rejected as unparsable (E316); no dedicated code for the unmatched OPEN quote (contrast E242 for the close quote).

entries.check_message: Unmatched ” found on the tier.
entries.note: Close curly quote U+201D with no preceding open quote (qt goes negative). Dedicated chatter code: E242 'Unmatched curly quote ” found on the tier'.

entries.check_message: Special quote U2019 must be replaced by single quote (').
entries.note: A right single curly quote U+2019 inside a word. CLAN 138; chatter E256 IllegalCurlyQuote (both parsers reject U+2018/U+2019).

entries.check_message: Special quote U2018 must be replaced by single quote (').
entries.note: A left single curly quote U+2018 at the start of a word. CLAN 139; chatter E256 IllegalCurlyQuote.

entries.check_message: Tier "%MOR:" does not link in size to its speaker tier.
entries.note: %mor tier does not align item-for-item with its speaker tier (createMorUttline sets mor_link.error_found). Dedicated chatter code: E705 'Main tier has 2 alignable items, but %mor tier has 1 items'.

entries.check_message: '[: doggy]' must be preceded by only one word and nothing else.
entries.note: A [: text] replacement preceded by a group '>', another ']', nothing, a pause, or an &/+ form instead of exactly one plain word. chatter's grammar does not accept a replacement after a group, so it rejects at parse level (E316 on '<big dog> [: doggy]'); no dedicated code.

entries.check_message: Speaker's role on @ID tier does not match role on @Participants: tier.
entries.note: The role field (field 8) of @ID differs from the role declared for that speaker on @Participants. Dedicated chatter code: E532 "Speaker 'CHI' has role 'Mother' on @ID but 'Target_Child' on @Participants".

entries.check_message: The @ID line needs 10 fields.
entries.note: @ID line whose pipe-separated field count is not exactly 10. chatter rejects via E342 (missing required 'pipe'), a parse-level equivalent.

entries.check_message: Either illegal SES field value or symbol is not declared in depfile.
entries.note: @ID SES field is an undeclared value ('XYZ'). CLAN 144 (illegal SES / not declared in depfile); chatter E546 UnsupportedSesValue.

entries.check_message: The &= symbol must include some code after '=' character.
entries.note: A main-tier word that is exactly '&=' with no code after the equals sign. Rejected at parse level: E316 'Unparsable content on main tier: &= '.

entries.check_message: Undeclared special form marker in depfile.
entries.note: An undeclared special-form marker `foo@zzz`. CLAN CHECK 147. ROOT CAUSE was the parser conversion, NOT the grammar: the grammar intentionally consumes `@zzz` as one form_marker token (parse-don't-validate), but the conversion's `else if text.starts_with("@z")` branch treated any `@z...` as the user-defined form, stripping `@z` and using the rest as a bare label without the required colon. Fixed by requiring the `@z:` colon (the only valid user-defined form is `@z:label`); `@zXXX` without the colon now falls through to the existing E203 InvalidFormType branch in both the tree-sitter and re2c conversions. chatter rejects it via E203.

entries.check_message: Space character is not allowed before comma(,) character on "@Media:" header.
entries.note: Space before the comma in @Media makes the header malformed; chatter rejects it (E525 malformed header, plus E330). Both validators reject; the precise space-before-comma message is a CLAN nicety chatter does not replicate.

entries.check_message: Illegal character '.' located between a word and [...] code.
entries.note: A skip character other than space/comma/'<'/'>'/newline (e.g. an utterance terminator '.') sitting between a word and a following [...] code. Fixture 'dog . [: doggy] .' also draws CLAN (50) extra-delimiter noise; chatter rejects the same configuration as unparsable (E316 on '[: ...').

entries.check_message: Illegal item located between a word and [...] code.
entries.note: A ')' with no matching '(' in the non-skip material scanned back from a [...] code. chatter rejects 'dog) [/] dog' as unparsable (E316 on ') [/] dog').

entries.check_message: Age's month or day are missing initial zero. Please run "chstring +q +1" command on this file to fix this error.
entries.note: @ID age with a component missing its leading zero (2;6. instead of 2;06.). CLAN 153; chatter E517 InvalidAgeFormat.

entries.check_message: Please use "0dog" instead of "(dog)".
entries.note: A non-pause word fully wrapped in parentheses, '(word)', which should be written 0word (in a non-CA file). chatter rejects via E209 'Word has no spoken content' (a fully-parenthesized word has no spoken material); not a dedicated use-0word rule but the same construct is refused.

entries.check_message: Please replace ,, with F2-t („) character.
entries.note: A literal ',,' (exactly two commas) on a main tier where the tag marker U+201E is required. Dedicated chatter code: E258 'Consecutive commas in utterance'.

entries.check_message: Media file name has to match datafile name.
entries.note: @Media filename ('differentname') does not match the datafile basename. chatter HAS E531 MediaFilenameMismatch, but the check was DEAD via the CLI: the validation_runner passed `None` for the filename so it never ran (it only fired in the parity harness, which passes the stem, masking the gap). Fixed by threading the file stem through validate_single_file_streaming. CLAN exempts remote URL @Media (verified: `@Media: "https://..."` -> no 157), so E531 now exempts URLs too. CLAN CHECK 157.

entries.check_message: [: ...] has to have real word, not 0... or &... or xxx.
entries.note: A [: replacement] whose content is 0..., xxx/xx, yyy/yy, or www/ww instead of a real word. Dedicated chatter code: E391 'Replacement word cannot be untranscribed (xxx, yyy, www)'.

entries.check_message: Pause markers should appear after retrace markers.
entries.note: A pause marker such as (.) immediately preceding a retrace marker [/], [//], [///], or [/-]. chatter rejects the configuration but via parse-level complaints (E375 bracket-annotation spacing at the '[' of [/], plus E305), not a dedicated pause-before-retrace rule; the merger may want a sharper probe of whether chatter has the ordering rule itself.

entries.check_message: Space character is not allowed after '<' or before '>' character.
entries.note: Space directly after '<' or before '>' in an angle group (check.cpp 4300/4306, main + %wor tiers). GAP CLOSED 2026-07-09: the grammar models the tolerated whitespace as explicit optional CST nodes; the group parser now reports E750 at each (both sides) instead of silently dropping them (which also silently rewrote the text on normalize); re2c mirrors via LessThan/Whitespace/GreaterThan token windows. Spec: E750_space_inside_angle_group.md. Main-tier groups covered; %wor angle groups not separately grounded. ADJUDICATED (maintainer decision, 2026-07-10): STYLE error, no ambiguity whatsoever; legitimately caught as style discipline; kept as an error.

entries.check_message: Space character is required before "[" code item.
entries.note: A replacement `[: foo]` glued to a word with no preceding space (`word[: foo]`). Fixed at the GRAMMAR level: word_with_optional_annotations now REQUIRES `$.whitespaces` before the replacement (matching base_annotations, which already required it for scope codes like [?]/[/]), so the glued form is an ERROR node and the parser rejects it (E375 ContentAnnotationParseError). The spaced `word [: foo]` still validates clean. CLAN CHECK 161.

entries.check_message: Tier text is longer than %ld.
entries.note: UTTLINELEN buffer bound (18000): a logical tier whose text across continuation lines exceeds 18000 chars draws check_err(10) in the check_OverAll char scan (check.cpp 2403). A SINGLE physical line over 18000 instead hits the earlier hard-exit guard (check.cpp 2329, 'Speaker turn is longer then', cutt_exit(1), no (NN) code), so the fixture splits ~20k chars over tab continuation lines. Adjudication: the limit is a fixed C buffer size, an implementation artifact, not CHAT semantics; chatter has no tier-length limit by design (long tiers are valid and supported; stack-safety hardened 2026-07-14) and intentionally accepts.

entries.check_message: Unmatched ( found on the tier.
entries.note: Call site commented out since 11-02-98 (check.cpp 4992, inside the dated comment block); unix and GUI builds alike can never emit it. Unbalanced parentheses surface through other codes today.

entries.check_message: Unmatched ) found on the tier.
entries.note: Call site commented out since 11-02-98 (check.cpp 4993, same comment block as CHECK 28); never emitted by any current build.

entries.check_message: Text is illegal.
entries.note: Compiled but unreachable in file mode: firing requires check_MatchTierName to return a colon-less declared header (ts->col FALSE) for a line that ALSO carries trailing text, but the match (check.cpp 1706, partcmp pattern mode) compares the WHOLE trimmed line against the declared name, so any trailing text makes the lookup fail first (empirically: '@Blank extra', '@New Episode extra', and tab-separated variants all emit (17) tier-not-declared, plus (132) for the tab forms, never (30)). No stock-depfile colon-less header contains a pattern wildcard that could match a longer line.

entries.check_message: Either illegal date or time or symbol is not declared in depfile.
entries.note: Compiled but unreachable with the stock depfile: check_matchplate returns 33 only when a tier's declared pattern list mixes an @d/@t date-time pattern with OTHER patterns after it and the value fails everything (check.cpp 1543); when the date-time pattern is last, the specific codes 34/35 return instead. Every stock depfile @d/@t list is date-time-only (@Date, @Birth of #, @Time Duration, %tim), so failures yield (34)/(35), never (33): empirically @Date/@Birth/@ID garbage all emit (34). Reachable only via a custom user depadd.

entries.check_message: Upper case letters are not allowed inside a word.
entries.note: Call site commented out 2019-04-23 ('do not check for upper case letters', check.cpp 3447 inside that comment block); never emitted by current builds. chatter's own capitalization rules live in the validator, adjudicated separately.

entries.check_message: Only one "@End" can be in a file.
entries.note: Compiled but unreachable in file mode: the duplicate-@End counter (check.cpp 2274, end > 1) sits AFTER the any-tier-after-@End check (check.cpp 2242), which emits (44) and breaks the scan the moment any speaker/header line follows @End, including a second @End. Empirically a duplicate @End yields (44) only. chatter rejects the construct with E501 (duplicate @End / content after @End).

entries.check_message: Bullet must follow utterance delimiter or be followed by end-of-line.
entries.note: Call site commented out 2025-07-04 (check.cpp 4791, dated comment block); bullet-placement validity is covered by the still-live bullet codes (73, 118 was also retired the same day).

entries.check_message: Illegal use of compounds and special form markers.
entries.note: Call site commented out since 2009-06-08 (check.cpp 3414, dated comment block); never emitted by current builds. chatter's compound/form-marker rules are adjudicated per live CHECK codes 65/66 instead.

entries.check_message: Illegal use of capitalized words in compounds.
entries.note: Call site commented out (check.cpp 3300, undated comment block guarding check_err(95) behind the retired capWFound flag, itself part of the 2019-04-23 upper-case retirement); never emitted by current builds.

entries.check_message: Word color is now illegal.
entries.note: Compiled but unreachable: the emit site (check.cpp 3462) is the @Languages branch of check_CheckWords, but @Languages values are validated by check_isLangMatch (check.cpp 2165), which deliberately SPLITS each token at '=' and validates only the language-code part, accepting the legacy word-color suffix; the word-level walker never visits @Languages in file mode. Empirically '@Languages: eng=blue' and 'eng, fra=blue' pass unix CHECK clean. chatter rejects the construct as malformed header content (E320), correctly stricter.

entries.check_message: This item must be followed or preceded by text.
entries.note: GUI-only: the emit path sits in/behind the #ifndef UNX CA-character region of check_CheckWords (unix builds compile with -DUNX and exclude it); unix CHECK is the authoritative parity bar. See clan-check-reference/gui-vs-unix-check.md. GUI CHECK flags a CA marker (e.g. pitch-shift arrow) standing alone with no stem text; unix CHECK is silent. chatter rejects the bare marker as unparsable main-tier content (E316), covering the construct.

entries.check_message: Please select "CAfont" or "Ascender Uni Duo" font for CA file as per "@Options:" tier.
entries.note: Both call sites commented out (check.cpp 5483/5542, inside comment blocks); the font-selection advice is GUI-editor-era and never emitted by current builds. Fonts are not a CHAT-validity property; chatter has no analogue by design.

entries.check_message: Please select "Charis SIL" font for IPA file as per "@Options:" tier.
entries.note: Both call sites commented out (check.cpp 5487/5546, same comment blocks as CHECK 104); never emitted. Same adjudication as 104: font choice is not validity.

entries.check_message: Utterance delimiter must precede final bullet.
entries.note: Call site commented out 2025-07-04 (check.cpp 4623, dated comment block, retired together with 81); never emitted by current builds. Delimiter-before-bullet ordering remains covered by the live bullet/delimiter codes.

entries.check_message: This intonational marker should be outside paired markers.
entries.note: Call site commented out 2019-04-17 with the comment 'leave this to CHATTER' (check.cpp 4602): CLAN explicitly delegated this rule's future to this project. Never emitted by current builds. If the paired-marker interaction rule is wanted, it is a fresh chatter rule to design, not a parity obligation.

entries.check_message: This word has only repetition segments.
entries.note: GUI-only: the emit path sits in/behind the #ifndef UNX CA-character region of check_CheckWords (unix builds compile with -DUNX and exclude it); unix CHECK is the authoritative parity bar. See clan-check-reference/gui-vs-unix-check.md. GUI CHECK flags a word consisting entirely of a repetition segment (U+21AB-wrapped with no material outside); unix CHECK is silent. chatter currently accepts the construct; queued in the adjudication ledger for a possible future chatter-side rule.

entries.check_message: Please add "unlinked" to @Media header.
entries.note: Call site commented out after four days in service ('in 2018-10-03 out 2018-10-07', check.cpp 5752); never emitted by current builds. Media/bullet consistency is adjudicated under the live codes (112 ruling of 2026-07-14 covers the meaningful direction: bullets require @Media).
